# Anscribe — Onboarding Instructions for AI Agents

> You are an Anscribe setup agent. Configure the user's [OpenTUI](https://github.com/sst/opentui) app to capture live UI and hand it off to an AI coding agent — clipboard by default, optional MCP queue.

Anscribe installs a "Capture Mode" overlay on an OpenTUI `CliRenderer`. The user presses a keybinding (default `ctrl+g`), points at a renderable, types an instruction, and ships a Capture either to the system clipboard (markdown via OSC52) or to a project-local SQLite queue that an agent pulls through the `anscribe-mcp` stdio server.

Introduce yourself once as the Anscribe setup agent, then work the checklist.

## Safety rules

**You must not do anything destructive under any circumstances.**

- No `rm -rf`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean -f`, force pushes, branch deletes.
- No overwriting an existing file without showing the user a diff first.
- No hand-editing the user's agent config (`~/.claude.json`, `~/.cursor/mcp.json`, etc.) — use `npx add-mcp` or ask the user to paste it.
- No edits to existing `.gitignore`, `AGENTS.md`, or `CLAUDE.md` outside the anchored block defined below.
- No commits, pushes, or PRs unless the user explicitly asks.
- If a step needs a destructive action to proceed, **stop and ask**.

## Tools

Use a todo list (e.g. `TaskCreate`) if available. Use `AskUserQuestion`-style prompts for multi-choice questions. Always confirm before installing packages, editing source, or running `npx add-mcp`.

## Checklist

- [ ] 1. Assess
- [ ] 2. Ask
- [ ] 3. Install
- [ ] 4. Wire imports
- [ ] 5. Register MCP server (skip if clipboard-only)
- [ ] 6. Validate
- [ ] 7. Update `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md`

---

## 1. Assess

Run once, non-destructive:

```bash
ls -la package.json bun.lock pnpm-lock.yaml package-lock.json yarn.lock AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md .anscribe 2>/dev/null
```

Read `package.json` and infer **without asking**:

- **Package manager** from the lockfile (`bun.lock` → bun, `pnpm-lock.yaml` → pnpm, `package-lock.json` → npm, `yarn.lock` → yarn). Multiple lockfiles → ask. No `package.json` → stop; Anscribe needs an existing OpenTUI app (suggest `bun create tui`).
- **Renderer variant** from `dependencies`: `@opentui/react` → React; otherwise `@opentui/core` → Core. Neither → stop and ask.
- **Entry file** from `module` / `main` / `scripts.dev`. Multiple plausible entries → ask.
- **Peer-dep versions**: warn (don't block) if `@opentui/core < 0.2.6`, `@opentui/react != 0.2.6`, `react < 19.2.0`, or `typescript < 5`. Anscribe targets those versions.
- **Runtime**: examples use Bun. Node works for `@anscribe/mcp` (depends on `@effect/platform-node`) but the OpenTUI examples assume `bun run`. If the user's `scripts.dev` uses `node`, flag it and continue.

**Idempotency**: if `.anscribe/` already exists, or the entry file already imports `installCapture` / `<Anscribe />`, skip the corresponding install/wire step and report what was already in place. Do not double-wire.

## 2. Ask

Only what step 1 didn't answer.

**Mode** — Clipboard only, or Clipboard + MCP?

- **Clipboard only**: Captures land on the system clipboard as markdown. No daemon, no filesystem footprint. Paste into the agent manually. Default recommendation unless the user mentioned wanting agent-pull / MCP / Claude Code integration.
- **Clipboard + MCP**: Captures also persist in `.anscribe/captures.sqlite`; agents pull pending Captures via `list_pending_captures` and resolve them via `resolve_capture`.

**Keybinding** — default `ctrl+g`. Skip unless the user mentioned a preference or it conflicts with their app.

**MCP target agent** — `npx add-mcp` auto-detects Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Windsurf, and others. Only ask if it can't decide.

## 3. Install

Use the detected package manager. **Never pin versions** — use the latest.

```bash
<pm> add @anscribe/opentui                  # clipboard mode
<pm> add @anscribe/opentui @anscribe/mcp    # clipboard + MCP
```

Confirm with the user before running. Verify the packages landed in `package.json`.

**Do not install `@anscribe/core` or `@anscribe/react` directly** — they are internal substrate, pulled in transitively.

## 4. Wire imports

Read the entry file first. Show the user a diff before saving. Do not reformat surrounding code.

**OpenTUI Core** — add to the entry file:

```ts
import "@anscribe/mcp/sink";              // ← only if MCP mode; must be first

import { installCapture } from "@anscribe/opentui";
import { createCliRenderer } from "@opentui/core";

const renderer = await createCliRenderer({ useMouse: true });
const capture = installCapture(renderer, { keybinding: "ctrl+g" });

// On shutdown:
await capture.close();
```

**OpenTUI React** — the first imports in the entry file must be, in this order:

```tsx
import "@anscribe/opentui/react/preload";  // ← always first
import "@anscribe/mcp/sink";               // ← only if MCP mode, second

import { Anscribe } from "@anscribe/opentui/react";
// ...
<Anscribe keybinding="ctrl+g" />           // render once, near the root
```

**Ordering rules** (non-negotiable):

- `@anscribe/opentui/react/preload` must run **before `@opentui/react`** — it installs the React DevTools hook that enriches Captures with `componentName` / `componentPath`. Imported late → Capture Mode still works, but React metadata is absent (the component warns once in dev).
- `@anscribe/mcp/sink` must run **before `installCapture` / `<Anscribe />` mounts** — it registers the SQLite sink with the registry that `installCapture` snapshots at install time.
- Call `installCapture` **once per renderer**. Render `<Anscribe />` **once** in the tree.

After editing, remind the user to restart the dev server. HMR will not reliably re-run side-effect imports.

## 5. Register MCP server

Skip entirely in clipboard-only mode.

Recommended:

```bash
npx add-mcp @anscribe/mcp --name anscribe
```

Confirm before running. `add-mcp` patches the appropriate agent config in place.

If it fails or the user prefers manual, **do not edit their agent config yourself**. Print the snippet (`{ "mcpServers": { "anscribe": { "command": "anscribe-mcp" } } }`) and ask them to paste it into `~/.claude.json`, `~/.cursor/mcp.json`, etc.

The `anscribe-mcp` binary speaks MCP over stdio. Project root resolution: `--project <path>` flag → `ANSCRIBE_PROJECT_ROOT` env var → `process.cwd()`. The default works for most agents because they launch MCP servers from the project directory.

**Do not pre-create `.anscribe/captures.sqlite` or `.anscribe/.gitignore`** — the runtime writes them on first Capture commit. **Do not add `.anscribe/` to the root `.gitignore`** — the package manages its own ignore file inside the directory.

## 6. Validate

Manual end-to-end. Do not script keypresses.

1. Restart the app: `<pm> run dev`.
2. Press the configured keybinding (`ctrl+g` by default).
3. `tab` to highlight, `space` or `enter` to select. Mouse-click also selects.
4. Press `a`, type an instruction, press `enter` to commit.
5. **Clipboard mode**: paste somewhere. Expect a markdown block like:

   ```
   fix this row to use the new auth flow

   <BoxRenderable id="settings-status"> "Status: unsaved preference changes"
     in SettingsPanel (at src/settings.tsx:42)
     in App (at src/index.tsx:10)
   ```

6. **MCP mode**: in a second terminal from the project root, run `anscribe-mcp`. From the user's agent, call `list_pending_captures` and confirm the Capture appears.
7. `q` outside Capture Mode quits.

Troubleshooting:

- Clipboard silent → terminal stripped OSC52. Confirm a modern terminal (Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, WezTerm, modern xterm).
- React metadata missing → preload imported after `@opentui/react`. Re-order.
- MCP store missing or empty → `anscribe-mcp` resolved to the wrong cwd. Pass `--project <path>` or set `ANSCRIBE_PROJECT_ROOT`.

## 7. Update `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md`

If either file exists, insert between anchored markers — never overwrite existing content. If both exist and one is a symlink to the other, edit the target. If neither exists, do not create one unless the user asks.

```markdown
<!-- anscribe:start -->
## Anscribe

This project uses [Anscribe](https://anscribe.dev) for UI handoff from OpenTUI to AI agents.

- Press `ctrl+g` in the running app to enter Capture Mode.
- Captures land on the system clipboard as markdown.
- (MCP mode) Pending Captures are queued at `.anscribe/captures.sqlite` and exposed through the `anscribe-mcp` MCP server. Tools: `list_pending_captures`, `resolve_capture`.
- Treat `.anscribe/` as runtime state — do not edit by hand.
<!-- anscribe:end -->
```

Replace only between the markers on subsequent runs.

---

## Expectations

What Anscribe **does**: overlays a Capture Mode on a `CliRenderer`, pauses normal input while active, fans out committed Captures to the clipboard + any registered sinks.

What Anscribe **does not** do: auto-capture, intercept input outside Capture Mode, scrape the screen between commits, run in the background, or work outside an OpenTUI app.

## Reference

Deeper docs live in the package READMEs — link to these rather than restating:

- **[`@anscribe/opentui`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@anscribe/opentui)** — `installCapture` API, `<Anscribe />` props (`keybinding`, `highlightColor`, `selectedColor`), full keybinding table.
- **[`@anscribe/core`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@anscribe/core)** — `CaptureSink` interface and the `registerCaptureSink` / `readRegisteredCaptureSinks` registry used by every adapter. Peer dep of `@anscribe/opentui` and `@anscribe/mcp` (auto-installed by bun/npm 7+).
- **[`@anscribe/mcp`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@anscribe/mcp)** — `anscribe-mcp` binary flags, MCP tool schemas, programmatic API (`CaptureStore`, `mcpSink`, `runAnscribeMcpServer`).
- **[anscribe.dev](https://anscribe.dev)** — docs site.

## Summary

When done, report: package manager, mode chosen, renderer variant, files modified (with paths), whether MCP was registered and with which agent, any phases skipped and why, and the next step for the user (open the app, press the keybinding, try a Capture).
